## Class DataSet<T>

  *     * org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet<T>

  * Type Parameters:
    `T` \- The type of the DataSet, i.e., the type of the elements of the DataSet.

Direct Known Subclasses:
    [BulkIterationResultSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/BulkIterationResultSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [DeltaIteration.SolutionSetPlaceHolder](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.SolutionSetPlaceHolder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [DeltaIteration.WorksetPlaceHolder](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.WorksetPlaceHolder.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [DeltaIterationResultSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIterationResultSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [NoOpOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/NoOpOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [Operator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/Operator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")

* * *




        [@Public](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/Public.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public abstract class DataSet<T>
        extends [Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

A DataSet represents a collection of elements of the same type.

A DataSet can be transformed into another DataSet by applying a transformation as for example

    * [`map(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.MapFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#map-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.MapFunction->),
    * [`reduce(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#reduce-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction->),
    * [`join(DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#join-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->), or
    * [`coGroup(DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#coGroup-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->).

      * #### fillInType

            protected void fillInType([TypeInformation](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeInformation.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> typeInfo)

Tries to fill in the type information. Type information can be filled in later when the program uses a type hint. This method checks whether the type information has ever been accessed before and does not allow modifications if the type was accessed already. This ensures consistency by making sure different parts of the operation do not assume different type information.

Parameters:
    `typeInfo` \- The type information to fill in.
Throws:
    `[IllegalStateException](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/IllegalStateException.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- Thrown, if the type information has been accessed before.
      * #### getType

Returns the [`TypeInformation`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeInformation.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo") for the type of this DataSet.

Returns:
    The TypeInformation for the type of this DataSet.
See Also:
    [`TypeInformation`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeInformation.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo")
      * #### clean

            public <F> F clean(F f)

      * #### map

            public <R> [MapOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/MapOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> map([MapFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/MapFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> mapper)

Applies a Map transformation on this DataSet.

The transformation calls a [`MapFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/MapFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") for each element of the DataSet. Each MapFunction call returns exactly one element.

Parameters:
    `mapper` \- The MapFunction that is called for each element of the DataSet.
Returns:
    A MapOperator that represents the transformed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`MapFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/MapFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`RichMapFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichMapFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`MapOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/MapOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
      * #### mapPartition

            public <R> [MapPartitionOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/MapPartitionOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> mapPartition([MapPartitionFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/MapPartitionFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> mapPartition)

Applies a Map-style operation to the entire partition of the data. The function is called once per parallel partition of the data, and the entire partition is available through the given Iterator. The number of elements that each instance of the MapPartition function sees is non deterministic and depends on the parallelism of the operation.

This function is intended for operations that cannot transform individual elements, requires no grouping of elements. To transform individual elements, the use of `map()` and `flatMap()` is preferable.

Parameters:
    `mapPartition` \- The MapPartitionFunction that is called for the full DataSet.
Returns:
    A MapPartitionOperator that represents the transformed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`MapPartitionFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/MapPartitionFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`MapPartitionOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/MapPartitionOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
      * #### flatMap

            public <R> [FlatMapOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/FlatMapOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> flatMap([FlatMapFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/FlatMapFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> flatMapper)

Applies a FlatMap transformation on a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

The transformation calls a [`RichFlatMapFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFlatMapFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") for each element of the DataSet. Each FlatMapFunction call can return any number of elements including none.

Parameters:
    `flatMapper` \- The FlatMapFunction that is called for each element of the DataSet.
Returns:
    A FlatMapOperator that represents the transformed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`RichFlatMapFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFlatMapFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`FlatMapOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/FlatMapOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### filter

            public [FilterOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/FilterOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> filter([FilterFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/FilterFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> filter)

Applies a Filter transformation on a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

The transformation calls a [`RichFilterFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFilterFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") for each element of the DataSet and retains only those element for which the function returns true. Elements for which the function returns false are filtered.

Parameters:
    `filter` \- The FilterFunction that is called for each element of the DataSet.
Returns:
    A FilterOperator that represents the filtered DataSet.
See Also:
    [`RichFilterFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFilterFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`FilterOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/FilterOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### project

            public <OUT extends [Tuple](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple")> [ProjectOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ProjectOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<?,OUT> project(int... fieldIndexes)

Applies a Project transformation on a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

**Note: Only Tuple DataSets can be projected using field indexes.**

The transformation projects each Tuple of the DataSet onto a (sub)set of fields.

Additional fields can be added to the projection by calling [`project(int[])`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#project-int...->).

**Note: With the current implementation, the Project transformation loses type information.**

Parameters:
    `fieldIndexes` \- The field indexes of the input tuple that are retained. The order of fields in the output tuple corresponds to the order of field indexes.
Returns:
    A ProjectOperator that represents the projected DataSet.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java"), [`ProjectOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ProjectOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
      * #### aggregate

            public [AggregateOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> aggregate([Aggregations](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/aggregation/Aggregations.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation") agg,
                                                  int field)

Applies an Aggregate transformation on a non-grouped [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

**Note: Only Tuple DataSets can be aggregated.** The transformation applies a built-in [`Aggregation`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/aggregation/Aggregations.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation") on a specified field of a Tuple DataSet. Additional aggregation functions can be added to the resulting [`AggregateOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") by calling [`AggregateOperator.and(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html#and-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->).

Parameters:
    `agg` \- The built-in aggregation function that is computed.
    `field` \- The index of the Tuple field on which the aggregation function is applied.
Returns:
    An AggregateOperator that represents the aggregated DataSet.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`Aggregations`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/aggregation/Aggregations.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation"), [`AggregateOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### sum

            public [AggregateOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> sum(int field)

Syntactic sugar for aggregate (SUM, field).

Parameters:
    `field` \- The index of the Tuple field on which the aggregation function is applied.
Returns:
    An AggregateOperator that represents the summed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`AggregateOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
      * #### max

            public [AggregateOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> max(int field)

Syntactic sugar for [`aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->) using [`Aggregations.MAX`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/aggregation/Aggregations.html#MAX>) as the aggregation function.

**Note:** This operation is not to be confused with [`maxBy(int...)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#maxBy-int...->), which selects one element with maximum value at the specified field positions.

Parameters:
    `field` \- The index of the Tuple field on which the aggregation function is applied.
Returns:
    An AggregateOperator that represents the max'ed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->), [`maxBy(int...)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#maxBy-int...->)
      * #### min

            public [AggregateOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> min(int field)

Syntactic sugar for [`aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->) using [`Aggregations.MIN`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/aggregation/Aggregations.html#MIN>) as the aggregation function.

**Note:** This operation is not to be confused with [`minBy(int...)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#minBy-int...->), which selects one element with the minimum value at the specified field positions.

Parameters:
    `field` \- The index of the Tuple field on which the aggregation function is applied.
Returns:
    An AggregateOperator that represents the min'ed DataSet.

      * #### reduce

            public [ReduceOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> reduce([ReduceFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/ReduceFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> reducer)

Applies a Reduce transformation on a non-grouped [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

The transformation consecutively calls a [`RichReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichReduceFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") until only a single element remains which is the result of the transformation. A ReduceFunction combines two elements into one new element of the same type.

Parameters:
    `reducer` \- The ReduceFunction that is applied on the DataSet.
Returns:
    A ReduceOperator that represents the reduced DataSet.
See Also:
    [`RichReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichReduceFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`ReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### reduceGroup

            public <R> [GroupReduceOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/GroupReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> reduceGroup([GroupReduceFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/GroupReduceFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> reducer)

Applies a GroupReduce transformation on a non-grouped [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

The transformation calls a [`RichGroupReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichGroupReduceFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") once with the full DataSet. The GroupReduceFunction can iterate over all elements of the DataSet and emit any number of output elements including none.

Parameters:
    `reducer` \- The GroupReduceFunction that is applied on the DataSet.
Returns:
    A GroupReduceOperator that represents the reduced DataSet.
See Also:
    [`RichGroupReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichGroupReduceFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`GroupReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/GroupReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### combineGroup

            public <R> [GroupCombineOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/GroupCombineOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> combineGroup([GroupCombineFunction](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/GroupCombineFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> combiner)

Applies a GroupCombineFunction on a non-grouped [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java"). A CombineFunction is similar to a GroupReduceFunction but does not perform a full data exchange. Instead, the CombineFunction calls the combine method once per partition for combining a group of results. This operator is suitable for combining values into an intermediate format before doing a proper groupReduce where the data is shuffled across the node for further reduction. The GroupReduce operator can also be supplied with a combiner by implementing the RichGroupReduce function. The combine method of the RichGroupReduce function demands input and output type to be the same. The CombineFunction, on the other side, can have an arbitrary output type.

Parameters:
    `combiner` \- The GroupCombineFunction that is applied on the DataSet.
Returns:
    A GroupCombineOperator which represents the combined DataSet.
      * #### minBy

            public [ReduceOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> minBy(int... fields)

Selects an element with minimum value.

The minimum is computed over the specified fields in lexicographical order.

**Example 1** : Given a data set with elements `[0, 1], [1, 0]`, the results will be:

        * `minBy(0)`: `[0, 1]`
        * `minBy(1)`: `[1, 0]`

**Example 2** : Given a data set with elements `[0, 0], [0, 1]`, the results will be:

        * `minBy(0, 1)`: `[0, 0]`

If multiple values with minimum value at the specified fields exist, a random one will be picked.

Internally, this operation is implemented as a [`ReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/ReduceFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions").

Parameters:
    `fields` \- Field positions to compute the minimum over
Returns:
    A [`ReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") representing the minimum
      * #### maxBy

            public [ReduceOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> maxBy(int... fields)

Selects an element with maximum value.

The maximum is computed over the specified fields in lexicographical order.

**Example 1** : Given a data set with elements `[0, 1], [1, 0]`, the results will be:

        * `maxBy(0)`: `[1, 0]`
        * `maxBy(1)`: `[0, 1]`

**Example 2** : Given a data set with elements `[0, 0], [0, 1]`, the results will be:

        * `maxBy(0, 1)`: `[0, 1]`

If multiple values with maximum value at the specified fields exist, a random one will be picked.

Internally, this operation is implemented as a [`ReduceFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/ReduceFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions").

Parameters:
    `fields` \- Field positions to compute the maximum over
Returns:
    A [`ReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") representing the maximum
      * #### first

            public [GroupReduceOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/GroupReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> first(int n)

Returns a new set containing the first n elements in this [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

Parameters:
    `n` \- The desired number of elements.
Returns:
    A ReduceGroupOperator that represents the DataSet containing the elements.
      * #### distinct

            public <K> [DistinctOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DistinctOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> distinct([KeySelector](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),K> keyExtractor)

Returns a distinct set of a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using a [`KeySelector`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions") function.

The KeySelector function is called for each element of the DataSet and extracts a single key value on which the decision is made if two items are distinct or not.

Parameters:
    `keyExtractor` \- The KeySelector function which extracts the key values from the DataSet on which the distinction of the DataSet is decided.
Returns:
    A DistinctOperator that represents the distinct DataSet.
      * #### distinct

            public [DistinctOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DistinctOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> distinct(int... fields)

Returns a distinct set of a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using field position keys.

The field position keys specify the fields of Tuples on which the decision is made if two Tuples are distinct or not.

Note: Field position keys can only be specified for Tuple DataSets.

Parameters:
    `fields` \- One or more field positions on which the distinction of the DataSet is decided.
Returns:
    A DistinctOperator that represents the distinct DataSet.
      * #### distinct

            public [DistinctOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DistinctOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> distinct([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")... fields)

Returns a distinct set of a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using expression keys.

The field expression keys specify the fields of a [`CompositeType`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeutils/CompositeType.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils") (e.g., Tuple or Pojo type) on which the decision is made if two elements are distinct or not. In case of a [`AtomicType`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/AtomicType.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo"), only the wildcard expression ("*") is valid.

Parameters:
    `fields` \- One or more field expressions on which the distinction of the DataSet is decided.
Returns:
    A DistinctOperator that represents the distinct DataSet.
      * #### distinct

            public [DistinctOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DistinctOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> distinct()

Returns a distinct set of a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java").

If the input is a [`CompositeType`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeutils/CompositeType.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils") (Tuple or Pojo type), distinct is performed on all fields and each field must be a key type

Returns:
    A DistinctOperator that represents the distinct DataSet.
      * #### groupBy

            public <K> [UnsortedGrouping](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> groupBy([KeySelector](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),K> keyExtractor)

Groups a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using a [`KeySelector`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions") function. The KeySelector function is called for each element of the DataSet and extracts a single key value on which the DataSet is grouped.

This method returns an [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the following grouping transformation can be applied.

        * [`UnsortedGrouping.sortGroup(int, org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#sortGroup-int-org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order->) to get a [`SortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/SortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators").
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->) to apply an Aggregate transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduce(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduce-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction->) to apply a Reduce transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduceGroup(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduceGroup-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction->) to apply a GroupReduce transformation.

Parameters:
    `keyExtractor` \- The [`KeySelector`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions") function which extracts the key values from the DataSet on which it is grouped.
Returns:
    An [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which a transformation needs to be applied to obtain a transformed DataSet.
See Also:
    [`KeySelector`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions"), [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`AggregateOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/AggregateOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`ReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/ReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`GroupReduceOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/GroupReduceOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### groupBy

            public [UnsortedGrouping](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> groupBy(int... fields)

Groups a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using field position keys.

**Note: Field position keys only be specified for Tuple DataSets.**

The field position keys specify the fields of Tuples on which the DataSet is grouped. This method returns an [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the following grouping transformation can be applied.

        * [`UnsortedGrouping.sortGroup(int, org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#sortGroup-int-org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order->) to get a [`SortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/SortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators").
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->) to apply an Aggregate transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduce(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduce-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction->) to apply a Reduce transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduceGroup(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduceGroup-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction->) to apply a GroupReduce transformation.

Parameters:

Returns:

Groups a [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") using field expressions. A field expression is either the name of a public field or a getter method with parentheses of the [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")S underlying type. A dot can be used to drill down into objects, as in `"field1.getInnerField2()" `. This method returns an [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the following grouping transformation can be applied.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.sortGroup(int, org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#sortGroup-int-org.apache.flink.api.common.operators.Order->) to get a [`SortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/SortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators").
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.aggregate(Aggregations, int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#aggregate-org.apache.flink.api.java.aggregation.Aggregations-int->) to apply an Aggregate transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduce(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduce-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction->) to apply a Reduce transformation.
        * [`UnsortedGrouping.reduceGroup(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html#reduceGroup-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.GroupReduceFunction->) to apply a GroupReduce transformation.

Parameters:
    `fields` \- One or more field expressions on which the DataSet will be grouped.
Returns:
    An [`UnsortedGrouping`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/UnsortedGrouping.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which a transformation needs to be applied to obtain a transformed DataSet.

            public <R> [JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> join([DataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")<R> other)

Initiates a Join transformation.

A Join transformation joins the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

This method returns a [`JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the `where` methods can be called to define the join key of the first joining (i.e., this) DataSet.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is joined.
Returns:
    A JoinOperatorSets to continue the definition of the Join transformation.
See Also:
    [`JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")

Initiates a Join transformation.

A Join transformation joins the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

This method returns a [`JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the `where` methods can be called to define the join key of the first joining (i.e., this) DataSet.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is joined.
    `strategy` \- The strategy that should be used execute the join. If `null` is given, then the optimizer will pick the join strategy.

Initiates a Join transformation.

A Join transformation joins the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

This method also gives the hint to the optimizer that the second DataSet to join is much smaller than the first one.

This method returns a [`JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which [`JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.where(String...)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/JoinOperator.JoinOperatorSets.html#where-java.lang.String...->) needs to be called to define the join key of the first joining (i.e., this) DataSet.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is joined.
Returns:
    A JoinOperatorSets to continue the definition of the Join transformation.

A Join transformation joins the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

Initiates a Left Outer Join transformation.

An Outer Join transformation joins two elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

Elements of the **left** DataSet (i.e. `this`) that do not have a matching element on the other side are joined with `null` and emitted to the resulting DataSet.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is joined.
Returns:
    A JoinOperatorSet to continue the definition of the Join transformation.

An Outer Join transformation joins two elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") on key equality and provides multiple ways to combine joining elements into one DataSet.

      * #### coGroup

            public <R> [CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> coGroup([DataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")<R> other)

Initiates a CoGroup transformation.

A CoGroup transformation combines the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") into one DataSet. It groups each DataSet individually on a key and gives groups of both DataSets with equal keys together into a [`RichCoGroupFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichCoGroupFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"). If a DataSet has a group with no matching key in the other DataSet, the CoGroupFunction is called with an empty group for the non-existing group.

The CoGroupFunction can iterate over the elements of both groups and return any number of elements including none.

This method returns a [`CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on which one of the `where` methods can be called to define the join key of the first joining (i.e., this) DataSet.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet of the CoGroup transformation.
Returns:
    A CoGroupOperatorSets to continue the definition of the CoGroup transformation.
See Also:
    [`CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CoGroupOperator.CoGroupOperatorSets.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`CoGroupOperator`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CoGroupOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")
      * #### cross

            public <R> [CrossOperator.DefaultCross](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> cross([DataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")<R> other)

Initiates a Cross transformation.

A Cross transformation combines the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") into one DataSet. It builds all pair combinations of elements of both DataSets, i.e., it builds a Cartesian product.

The resulting [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") wraps each pair of crossed elements into a [`Tuple2`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple2.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), with the element of the first input being the first field of the tuple and the element of the second input being the second field of the tuple.

Call [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross.with(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html#with-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction->) to define a [`CrossFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/CrossFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") which is called for each pair of crossed elements. The CrossFunction returns a exactly one element for each pair of input elements.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is crossed.
Returns:
    A DefaultCross that returns a Tuple2 for each pair of crossed elements.
See Also:
    [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`CrossFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/CrossFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java"), [`Tuple2`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple2.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple")
      * #### crossWithTiny

            public <R> [CrossOperator.DefaultCross](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> crossWithTiny([DataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")<R> other)

Initiates a Cross transformation.

A Cross transformation combines the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") into one DataSet. It builds all pair combinations of elements of both DataSets, i.e., it builds a Cartesian product. This method also gives the hint to the optimizer that the second DataSet to cross is much smaller than the first one.

The resulting [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") wraps each pair of crossed elements into a [`Tuple2`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple2.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), with the element of the first input being the first field of the tuple and the element of the second input being the second field of the tuple.

Call [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross.with(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html#with-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction->) to define a [`CrossFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/CrossFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") which is called for each pair of crossed elements. The CrossFunction returns a exactly one element for each pair of input elements.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is crossed.
Returns:
    A DefaultCross that returns a Tuple2 for each pair of crossed elements.
See Also:

Initiates a Cross transformation.

A Cross transformation combines the elements of two [`DataSets`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java") into one DataSet. It builds all pair combinations of elements of both DataSets, i.e., it builds a Cartesian product. This method also gives the hint to the optimizer that the second DataSet to cross is much larger than the first one.

The resulting [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") wraps each pair of crossed elements into a [`Tuple2`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple2.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), with the element of the first input being the first field of the tuple and the element of the second input being the second field of the tuple.

Call [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross.with(org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html#with-org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.CrossFunction->) to define a [`CrossFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/CrossFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions") which is called for each pair of crossed elements. The CrossFunction returns a exactly one element for each pair of input elements.

Parameters:
    `other` \- The other DataSet with which this DataSet is crossed.
Returns:
    A DefaultCross that returns a Tuple2 for each pair of crossed elements.
See Also:
    [`CrossOperator.DefaultCross`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CrossOperator.DefaultCross.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators"), [`CrossFunction`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/CrossFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [`DataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java"), [`Tuple2`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple2.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple")
      * #### iterate

            public [IterativeDataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/IterativeDataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> iterate(int maxIterations)

Initiates an iterative part of the program that executes multiple times and feeds back data sets. The iterative part needs to be closed by calling [`IterativeDataSet.closeWith(DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/IterativeDataSet.html#closeWith-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->). The data set given to the `closeWith(DataSet)` method is the data set that will be fed back and used as the input to the next iteration. The return value of the `closeWith(DataSet)` method is the resulting data set after the iteration has terminated.

An example of an iterative computation is as follows:

            DataSet<Double> input = ...;

             DataSet<Double> startOfIteration = input.iterate(10);
             DataSet<Double> toBeFedBack = startOfIteration
                                           .map(new MyMapper())
                                           .groupBy(...).reduceGroup(new MyReducer());
             DataSet<Double> result = startOfIteration.closeWith(toBeFedBack);


The iteration has a maximum number of times that it executes. A dynamic termination can be realized by using a termination criterion (see [`IterativeDataSet.closeWith(DataSet, DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/IterativeDataSet.html#closeWith-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->)).

Parameters:
    `maxIterations` \- The maximum number of times that the iteration is executed.
Returns:
    An IterativeDataSet that marks the start of the iterative part and needs to be closed by [`IterativeDataSet.closeWith(DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/IterativeDataSet.html#closeWith-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->).
See Also:
    [`IterativeDataSet`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/IterativeDataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
      * #### iterateDelta

            public <R> [DeltaIteration](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),R> iterateDelta([DataSet](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java")<R> workset,
                                                        int maxIterations,
                                                        int... keyPositions)

Initiates a delta iteration. A delta iteration is similar to a regular iteration (as started by [`iterate(int)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#iterate-int->), but maintains state across the individual iteration steps. The Solution set, which represents the current state at the beginning of each iteration can be obtained via [`DeltaIteration.getSolutionSet()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.html#getSolutionSet-->) ()}. It can be be accessed by joining (or CoGrouping) with it. The DataSet that represents the workset of an iteration can be obtained via [`DeltaIteration.getWorkset()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.html#getWorkset-->). The solution set is updated by producing a delta for it, which is merged into the solution set at the end of each iteration step.

The delta iteration must be closed by calling [`DeltaIteration.closeWith(DataSet, DataSet)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.html#closeWith-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet-org.apache.flink.api.java.DataSet->). The two parameters are the delta for the solution set and the new workset (the data set that will be fed back). The return value of the `closeWith(DataSet, DataSet)` method is the resulting data set after the iteration has terminated. Delta iterations terminate when the feed back data set (the workset) is empty. In addition, a maximum number of steps is given as a fall back termination guard.

Elements in the solution set are uniquely identified by a key. When merging the solution set delta, contained elements with the same key are replaced.

**NOTE:** Delta iterations currently support only tuple valued data types. This restriction will be removed in the future. The key is specified by the tuple position.

A code example for a delta iteration is as follows

            DeltaIteration<Tuple2<Long, Long>, Tuple2<Long, Long>> iteration =
                                                              initialState.iterateDelta(initialFeedbackSet, 100, 0);

             DataSet<Tuple2<Long, Long>> delta = iteration.groupBy(0).aggregate(Aggregations.AVG, 1)
                                                          .join(iteration.getSolutionSet()).where(0).equalTo(0)
                                                          .flatMap(new ProjectAndFilter());

             DataSet<Tuple2<Long, Long>> feedBack = delta.join(someOtherSet).where(...).equalTo(...).with(...);

             // close the delta iteration (delta and new workset are identical)
             DataSet<Tuple2<Long, Long>> result = iteration.closeWith(delta, feedBack);


Parameters:
    `workset` \- The initial version of the data set that is fed back to the next iteration step (the workset).
    `maxIterations` \- The maximum number of iteration steps, as a fall back safeguard.
    `keyPositions` \- The position of the tuple fields that is used as the key of the solution set.
Returns:
    The DeltaIteration that marks the start of a delta iteration.
See Also:
    [`DeltaIteration`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DeltaIteration.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")

Runs a [`CustomUnaryOperation`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/CustomUnaryOperation.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators") on the data set. Custom operations are typically complex operators that are composed of multiple steps.

Parameters:
    `operation` \- The operation to run.
Returns:
    The data set produced by the operation.

      * #### rebalance

            public [PartitionOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/PartitionOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> rebalance()

Enforces a re-balancing of the DataSet, i.e., the DataSet is evenly distributed over all parallel instances of the following task. This can help to improve performance in case of heavy data skew and compute intensive operations.

**Important:** This operation shuffles the whole DataSet over the network and can take significant amount of time.

Returns:
    The re-balanced DataSet.

            public <K> [SortPartitionOperator](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/SortPartitionOperator.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> sortPartition([KeySelector](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/functions/KeySelector.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.java.functions")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet"),K> keyExtractor,
                                                              [Order](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/operators/Order.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.api.common.operators") order)

Locally sorts the partitions of the DataSet on the extracted key in the specified order. The DataSet can be sorted on multiple values by returning a tuple from the KeySelector.

Note that no additional sort keys can be appended to a KeySelector sort keys. To sort the partitions by multiple values using KeySelector, the KeySelector must return a tuple consisting of the values.

Parameters:
    `keyExtractor` \- The KeySelector function which extracts the key values from the DataSet on which the DataSet is sorted.
    `order` \- The order in which the DataSet is sorted.
Returns:
    The DataSet with sorted local partitions.
      * #### writeAsText

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> writeAsText([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath)

Writes a DataSet as text file(s) to the specified location.

For each element of the DataSet the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

Output files and directories
What output how writeAsText() method produces is depending on other circumstance

        * A directory is created and multiple files are written underneath. (Default behavior)
This sink creates a directory called "path1", and files "1", "2" ... are written underneath depending on [parallelism](<https://flink.apache.org/faq.html#what-is-the-parallelism-how-do-i-set-it>)

              .
               └── path1/
                   ├── 1
                   ├── 2
                   └── ...

Code Example

              dataset.writeAsText("file:///path1");

        * A single file called "path1" is created when parallelism is set to 1

              .
               └── path1

Code Example

              // Parallelism is set to only this particular operation
               dataset.writeAsText("file:///path1").setParallelism(1);

               // This will creates the same effect but note all operators' parallelism are set to one
               env.setParallelism(1);
               ...
               dataset.writeAsText("file:///path1");

        * A directory is always created when [fs.output.always-create-directory](<https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-master/setup/config.html#file-systems>) is set to true in flink-conf.yaml file, even when parallelism is set to 1.

              .
               └── path1/
                   └── 1

Code Example

              // fs.output.always-create-directory = true
               dataset.writeAsText("file:///path1").setParallelism(1);

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path pointing to the location the text file or files under the directory is written to.
Returns:
    The DataSink that writes the DataSet.
See Also:
    [`TextOutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/io/TextOutputFormat.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.io")
      * #### writeAsText

                                           [FileSystem.WriteMode](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/FileSystem.WriteMode.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.core.fs") writeMode)

Writes a DataSet as text file(s) to the specified location.

For each element of the DataSet the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

Parameters:

      * #### writeAsCsv

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> writeAsCsv([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath)

Writes a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") DataSet as CSV file(s) to the specified location.

**Note: Only a Tuple DataSet can written as a CSV file.**

For each Tuple field the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written. Tuple fields are separated by the default field delimiter `"comma" (,)`.

Tuples are are separated by the newline character (`\n`).

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path pointing to the location the CSV file is written to.
Returns:
    The DataSink that writes the DataSet.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`CsvOutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/io/CsvOutputFormat.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.io"), [`Output files and directories`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#writeAsText-java.lang.String->)
      * #### writeAsCsv

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> writeAsCsv([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath,
                                          [FileSystem.WriteMode](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/core/fs/FileSystem.WriteMode.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.core.fs") writeMode)

Writes a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") DataSet as CSV file(s) to the specified location.

**Note: Only a Tuple DataSet can written as a CSV file.**

For each Tuple field the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written. Tuple fields are separated by the default field delimiter `"comma" (,)`.

Tuples are are separated by the newline character (`\n`).

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path pointing to the location the CSV file is written to.
    `writeMode` \- The behavior regarding existing files. Options are NO_OVERWRITE and OVERWRITE.
Returns:
    The DataSink that writes the DataSet.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`CsvOutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/io/CsvOutputFormat.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.io"), [`Output files and directories`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#writeAsText-java.lang.String->)
      * #### writeAsCsv

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> writeAsCsv([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") filePath,

Writes a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") DataSet as CSV file(s) to the specified location with the specified field and line delimiters.

**Note: Only a Tuple DataSet can written as a CSV file.**

For each Tuple field the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path pointing to the location the CSV file is written to.
    `rowDelimiter` \- The row delimiter to separate Tuples.
    `fieldDelimiter` \- The field delimiter to separate Tuple fields.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`CsvOutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/io/CsvOutputFormat.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.io"), [`Output files and directories`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#writeAsText-java.lang.String->)

Writes a [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple") DataSet as CSV file(s) to the specified location with the specified field and line delimiters.

**Note: Only a Tuple DataSet can written as a CSV file.** For each Tuple field the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

Parameters:
    `filePath` \- The path pointing to the location the CSV file is written to.
    `rowDelimiter` \- The row delimiter to separate Tuples.
    `fieldDelimiter` \- The field delimiter to separate Tuple fields.
    `writeMode` \- The behavior regarding existing files. Options are NO_OVERWRITE and OVERWRITE.
See Also:
    [`Tuple`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/tuple/Tuple.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple"), [`CsvOutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/io/CsvOutputFormat.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.io"), [`Output files and directories`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#writeAsText-java.lang.String->)
      * #### print

            public void print()
                       throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Prints the elements in a DataSet to the standard output stream [`System.out`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/System.html?is-external=true#out> "class or interface in java.lang") of the JVM that calls the print() method. For programs that are executed in a cluster, this method needs to gather the contents of the DataSet back to the client, to print it there.

The string written for each element is defined by the [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") method.

This method immediately triggers the program execution, similar to the [`collect()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#collect-->) and [`count()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#count-->) methods.

Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
See Also:
    [`printToErr()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#printToErr-->), [`printOnTaskManager(String)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#printOnTaskManager-java.lang.String->)
      * #### printToErr

            public void printToErr()
                            throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Prints the elements in a DataSet to the standard error stream [`System.err`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/System.html?is-external=true#err> "class or interface in java.lang") of the JVM that calls the print() method. For programs that are executed in a cluster, this method needs to gather the contents of the DataSet back to the client, to print it there.

The string written for each element is defined by the [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") method.

This method immediately triggers the program execution, similar to the [`collect()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#collect-->) and [`count()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#count-->) methods.

Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
See Also:
    [`print()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#print-->), [`printOnTaskManager(String)`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#printOnTaskManager-java.lang.String->)
      * #### printOnTaskManager

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> printOnTaskManager([String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") prefix)

Writes a DataSet to the standard output streams (stdout) of the TaskManagers that execute the program (or more specifically, the data sink operators). On a typical cluster setup, the data will appear in the TaskManagers' _.out_ files.

To print the data to the console or stdout stream of the client process instead, use the [`print()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#print-->) method.

For each element of the DataSet the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

Parameters:
    `prefix` \- The string to prefix each line of the output with. This helps identifying outputs from different printing sinks.
Returns:
    The DataSink operator that writes the DataSet.
See Also:
    [`print()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html#print-->)
      * #### print

For each element of the DataSet the result of [`Object.toString()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true#toString--> "class or interface in java.lang") is written.

    `sinkIdentifier` \- The string to prefix the output with.

      * #### output

            public [DataSink](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> output([OutputFormat](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/io/OutputFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.io")<[T](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/DataSet.html> "type parameter in DataSet")> outputFormat)

Emits a DataSet using an [`OutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/io/OutputFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.io"). This method adds a data sink to the program. Programs may have multiple data sinks. A DataSet may also have multiple consumers (data sinks or transformations) at the same time.

Parameters:
    `outputFormat` \- The OutputFormat to process the DataSet.
Returns:
    The DataSink that processes the DataSet.
See Also:
    [`OutputFormat`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/io/OutputFormat.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.io"), [`DataSink`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/java/operators/DataSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.java.operators")
